title: Full pipeline smoke test: engineer to test to review
description:
Goal: verify that the repaired multi-agent pipeline performs a real multi-stage task and that the 
dashboard stages correspond to real execution by the appropriate worker/stage.

Scope:
- make one small bounded code or documentation change in rook-workspace that is substantial enough to justify engineer work, testing, and review
- preferred change: add one short verification subsection to docs/RUNTIME-OPERATIONS.md called "Stage Transition Validation" explaining that:
  - Ready means dispatchable but not yet claimed
  - In Progress means engineer execution is active
  - Testing means test-stage work is active
  - Review means review-stage work is active
  - Done means the task completed honestly and the board/canonical state are aligned
- if a better small change is needed to force a real test/review path, the worker may choose a similarly small bounded change in rook-workspace
- commit using normal agent/task commit prefixes
- update canonical task state honestly at each stage
- post important lifecycle messages in Discord

Success criteria:
- task starts in Ready
- task moves to In Progress during engineer execution
- task moves to Testing during test-stage execution
- task moves to Review during review-stage execution
- task ends in Done only after real completion
- Discord shows important lifecycle updates
- canonical task state matches the visible board state
- Git artifacts exist: branch and commit, and PR if applicable
- if anything fails, task becomes blocked honestly with a real reason

Suggested task metadata:
- repo/project: rook-workspace
- status: Ready
- assigned agent: rook or coordinator-owned
- priority: normal
